evals.prompt: I have an existing products table in my NocoDB-connected PostgreSQL database. I need to: rename the 'name' column to 'title', change the 'price' column from int4 to decimal(10,2), and add a new 'description' text column. Show me the SQL.
evals.expected_output: Three ALTER TABLE statements: RENAME COLUMN, ALTER COLUMN TYPE with USING cast, ADD column. Correct PostgreSQL syntax with quoted identifiers.

expectations.text: Uses ALTER TABLE ... RENAME COLUMN "name" TO "title" syntax
Uses ALTER COLUMN ... TYPE decimal(10,2) USING cast syntax
Drops default before changing type (ALTER COLUMN ... DROP DEFAULT)
Uses ALTER TABLE ... ADD "description" text syntax
Uses double-quoted identifiers for table and column names

evals.prompt: I need to completely remove a many-to-many relationship between products and tags in my PostgreSQL database. The junction table is nc_m2m_products_tags. What's the safe order of operations?
evals.expected_output: Drop FK constraints first (both fk_products_tags_1 and fk_products_tags_2), then DROP TABLE. Shows the correct teardown order.

expectations.text: Drops FK constraints before dropping the table
Drops both FK constraints (two ALTER TABLE DROP CONSTRAINT statements)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS comes after constraint removal
Shows operations in the correct safe order: constraints first, then table
